tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I wanted to talk about saving some of my preferences. I'm a huge football fan - support Manchester United actually, been following them since I was a kid. And I've always had a thing for architecture too, especially Art Deco buildings, there's something about those geometric patterns that just fascinates me. Can you help me remember these things?
I'd be happy to help you remember those things! Let me save your preferences now.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User is a huge football fan and supports Manchester United. They've been following the club since they were a kid. Additionally, they have a strong interest in architecture, particularly Art Deco buildings, fascinated by their geometric patterns."}

Additionally, they have a strong interest in architecture, particularly Art Deco buildings, fascinated by their geometric patterns."}}

Done! I've saved your preferences:

- **Football**: You're a Manchester United supporter, and you've been following them since you were a kid
- **Architecture**: You have a passion for Art Deco buildings, especially fascinated by their geometric patterns

I'll remember these for future reference. Anything else you'd like me to note?

Hey, that's great! Quick question though - can you double-check that you got my football preference right? I want to make sure it's saved correctly since I can be a bit forgetful sometimes. What exactly did you write down about me supporting Manchester United?

messages.content:
Here's everything I've stored for you so far:

**Memory (ID: m2):**
> User is a huge football fan and supports Manchester United. They've been following the club since they were a kid. Additionally, they have a strong interest in architecture, particularly Art Deco buildings, fascinated by their geometric patterns.

Right now you just have the one consolidated memory that captures both your football and architecture interests. If you'd like me to split these into separate memories or add anything else, just let me know!

Hey, so I was looking at that memory you saved, and I think there's something I need to fix. I actually don't think I'm into Art Deco anymore - I've changed my mind over the years and now I'm more into Victorian architecture instead. Could you delete that part about the Art Deco buildings from my memory? I'd rather just keep the football stuff or update it with my new architectural preference.
Good catch! I can update that for you. Since the delete function works on whole memories, I'll remove the old one and save a fresh, updated version with your new preference.

You're welcome! Glad we got everything updated correctly — your Manchester United support and your Victorian architecture interest are now safely stored. If you ever want to add more preferences or make changes, just let me know. Enjoy the rest of your day!
